title: Gloriously Dense Mexican Borlotti Beans Skillet
description: A resinous, dense, solo-dinner Mexican dish featuring borlotti beans and bhuna, perfect for a keto-friendly meal.
instructions: Heat a large skillet over medium-high heat and add the ground beef, cooking until browned, breaking it up into small pieces as it cooks.
Add the chopped onion to the skillet and cook until it is translucent and starting to caramelize.
Add the minced garlic to the skillet and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Stir in the bhuna spice mix and cook for 1 minute, until the spices are fragrant.
Add the cooked borlotti beans, tomato paste, and chicken broth to the skillet, stirring to combine.
Bring the mixture to a simmer and cook, stirring occasionally, until the liquid has thickened and the flavors have melded together.
Taste and adjust the seasoning as needed.
If using cheese, sprinkle it over the top of the skillet and cook until melted and bubbly.
Garnish with chopped cilantro and serve hot.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
Cook ground beef to an internal temperature of 71°C / 160°F (no longer pink in the middle) before serving.
